what: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
title: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
text: tabpfn, meaning prior fitted networks tabular data, deep-learning model. See: Transformers Can Bayesian Inference (arXiv, 2021) TabPFN: Transformer Solves Small Tabular Classification Problems Second (arXiv, 2022) Accurate predictions small data tabular foundation model (Nature, 2025) R package wrapper Python library via reticulate. idiomatic R syntax using standard S3 methods.

title: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
text: can download package CRAN via: can install development version tabpfn like : ’ll need Python virtual environment access underlying library. installing R package, tabpfn install required Python bits first fit model:
code: install.packages("tabpfn") require(pak) pak(c("tidymodels/tabpfn"), ask = FALSE) > library(tabpfn) > > predictors <- mtcars[, -1] > outcome <- mtcars[, 1] > > # XY interface > mod <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome) Downloading uv...Done! Downloading cpython-3.12.12 (download) (15.9MiB)  Downloading cpython-3.12.12 (download) Downloading setuptools (1.1MiB) Downloading scikit-learn (8.2MiB) Downloading numpy (4.9MiB)  <downloading and installing more packages>   Downloading llvmlite  Downloading torch Installed 58 packages in 350ms > mod tabpfn Regression Model  Training set i 32 data points i 10 predictors

title: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
text: loading package: can fit model via standard x/y interface. also formula recipes interfaces. Prediction follows usual S3 predict() method: tabpfn follows tidymodels prediction convention: data frame always returned standard set column names. classification model, outcome always factor vector. example, using data modeldata package: fit looks fairly good shown --sample data:
code: library(tabpfn) set.seed(364) reg_mod <- tab_pfn(mtcars[1:25, -1], mtcars$mpg[1:25]) reg_mod #> TabPFN Regression Model #> Training set #> ℹ 25 data points #> ℹ 10 predictors predict(reg_mod, mtcars[26:32, -1]) #> # A tibble: 7 × 1 #>   .pred #>   <dbl> #> 1  31.4 #> 2  24.3 #> 3  24.8 #> 4  16.4 #> 5  18.9 #> 6  14.4 #> 7  22.5 library(modeldata) #>  #> Attaching package: 'modeldata' #> The following object is masked from 'package:datasets': #>  #>     penguins library(ggplot2)  two_cls_train <- parabolic[1:400,  ] two_cls_val   <- parabolic[401:500,] grid <- expand.grid(X1 = seq(-5.1, 5.0, length.out = 25),                      X2 = seq(-5.5, 4.0, length.out = 25))  set.seed(3824) cls_mod <- tab_pfn(class ~ ., data = two_cls_train)  grid_pred <- predict(cls_mod, grid) grid_pred #> # A tibble: 625 × 3 #>    .pred_Class1 .pred_Class2 .pred_class #>           <dbl>        <dbl> <fct>       #>  1        0.997      0.00273 Class1      #>  2        0.998      0.00217 Class1      #>  3        0.998      0.00182 Class1      #>  4        0.998      0.00155 Class1      #>  5        0.998      0.00167 Class1      #>  6        0.998      0.00222 Class1      #>  7        0.996      0.00438 Class1      #>  8        0.989      0.0109  Class1      #>  9        0.948      0.0522  Class1      #> 10        0.745      0.255   Class1      #> # ℹ 615 more rows cbind(grid, grid_pred) |>   ggplot(aes(X1, X2)) +   geom_point(     data = two_cls_val,     aes(col = class, pch = class),     alpha = 3 / 4,     cex = 3   ) +   geom_contour(     aes(z = .pred_Class1),     breaks = 1 / 2,     col = "black",     linewidth = 1   ) +   coord_equal(ratio = 1)

title: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
text: PriorLabs created model. Starting version 2.5, using TabPFN requires accepting model license setting token. model version (v2.5, v2.6, etc.) license must accepted individually. get access, visit https://ux.priorlabs.ai, go Licenses tab (1), accept license model version intend use (2). set TABPFN_TOKEN environment variable token account. Users already TABPFN_TOKEN set can use TabPFN v2 without additional steps. Screenshot PriorLabs UX portal Licenses page Also, model effective GPU available (order magnitude two). may seem obvious anyone already working deep learning models, fairly new requirement strictly working traditional tabular data models.

title: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R
text: Please note tabpfn project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Controlling TabICL execution — control_tab_icl
text: Controlling TabICL execution

title: Controlling TabICL execution — control_tab_icl
code: control_tab_icl(   n_estimators = 8L,   norm_methods = NULL,   feat_shuffle_method = "latin",   class_shuffle_method = "shift",   outlier_threshold = 4,   softmax_temperature = 0.9,   average_logits = TRUE,   support_many_classes = TRUE,   batch_size = 8L,   kv_cache = FALSE,   model_path = NULL,   allow_auto_download = TRUE,   checkpoint_version = NULL,   device = NULL,   use_amp = "auto",   use_fa3 = "auto",   offload_mode = "auto",   disk_offload_dir = NULL,   random_state = 42L,   n_jobs = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   inference_config = NULL,   ... )

title: Controlling TabICL execution — control_tab_icl
text: n_estimators Number ensemble members. norm_methods Normalization methods passed Python constructor. feat_shuffle_method Feature permutation strategy. class_shuffle_method Class permutation strategy classification. outlier_threshold Z-score threshold outlier clipping. softmax_temperature Temperature classification probabilities. average_logits Whether average logits probabilities. support_many_classes Whether enable TabICL's many-class support. batch_size Number ensemble members processed together. kv_cache Whether cache key-value projections repeated inference. model_path Optional local checkpoint path. allow_auto_download Whether Python package may download checkpoints. checkpoint_version Optional checkpoint version. device Inference device, "cpu", "cuda", NULL. use_amp Automatic mixed precision setting. use_fa3 Flash Attention 3 setting. offload_mode CPU/disk offloading mode. disk_offload_dir Optional disk offload directory. random_state Random seed. n_jobs Number PyTorch CPU threads. verbose Whether Python print inference details. inference_config Optional fine-grained Python inference config. ... Additional named arguments passed Python constructor.

title: Controlling TabICL execution — control_tab_icl
text: list class "control_tab_icl".

title: Controlling TabPFN execution — control_tab_pfn
text: Controlling TabPFN execution

title: Controlling TabPFN execution — control_tab_pfn
code: control_tab_pfn(   n_preprocessing_jobs = 1L,   device = "auto",   ignore_pretraining_limits = FALSE,   inference_precision = "auto",   fit_mode = "fit_preprocessors",   memory_saving_mode = "auto",   random_state = sample.int(10^6, 1),   ... )

title: Controlling TabPFN execution — control_tab_pfn
text: n_preprocessing_jobs integer number worker processes. value -1L indicates possible resources. device character value device used torch (e.g., "cpu", "cuda", "mps", etc.). Th default "auto". ignore_pretraining_limits logical bypass default data limits :number training set samples (10,000) , number predictors (500). unchangeable limit number classes (10). inference_precision character value trade speed reproducibility. can torch dtype, "autocast" (torch's mixed-precision autocast), "auto". fit_mode character value control preprocessed /cached. Values "fit_preprocessors" (default), "low_memory", "fit_with_cache", "batched". memory_saving_mode character string help --memory errors. Values either logical "auto". random_state integer set random number stream. ... Additional named arguments passed directly TabPFN Python constructor. Use supply options covered named parameters (e.g. arguments added newer versions Python package).

title: Controlling TabPFN execution — control_tab_pfn
text: list extra class "control_tab_pfn" named elements argument values.

title: Predict using TabPFN — predict.tab_pfn
code: # Minimal example for quick execution car_train <- mtcars[ 1:5,   ] car_test  <- mtcars[6, -1]  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fit if (is_tab_pfn_installed() & interactive()) {  mod <- tab_pfn(mpg ~ cyl + log(drat), car_train)   # Predict  predict(mod, car_test)  augment(mod, car_test) } } # }

title: Fit a TabICLv2 model — tab_icl
text: tab_icl() fits Python tabicl classifier regressor reticulate returns R object predict() augment() methods.

title: Fit a TabICLv2 model — tab_icl
code: tab_icl(x, ...)  # Default S3 method tab_icl(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' tab_icl(x, y, training_set_limit = Inf, control = control_tab_icl(), ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' tab_icl(x, y, training_set_limit = Inf, control = control_tab_icl(), ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' tab_icl(   formula,   data,   training_set_limit = Inf,   control = control_tab_icl(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'recipe' tab_icl(x, data, training_set_limit = Inf, control = control_tab_icl(), ...)

title: Fit a TabICLv2 model — tab_icl
text: x data frame, matrix, recipe, formula. ... currently used. y Outcome vector data-frame matrix interfaces. training_set_limit Maximum number training rows retained fitting. Use Inf disable downsampling. control list control_tab_icl(). formula Formula formula interface. data Data frame formula recipe interfaces.

title: Fit a TabICLv2 model — tab_icl
text: tab_icl model object.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: tab_pfn() applies data pre-estimated deep learning model defined Hollmann et al (2025). model emulates Bayesian inference regression classification models.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
code: tab_pfn(x, ...)  # Default S3 method tab_pfn(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' tab_pfn(   x,   y,   num_estimators = 8L,   softmax_temperature = 0.9,   balance_probabilities = FALSE,   average_before_softmax = FALSE,   training_set_limit = 10000,   version = NULL,   control = control_tab_pfn(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'matrix' tab_pfn(   x,   y,   num_estimators = 8L,   softmax_temperature = 0.9,   balance_probabilities = FALSE,   average_before_softmax = FALSE,   training_set_limit = 10000,   version = NULL,   control = control_tab_pfn(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'formula' tab_pfn(   formula,   data,   num_estimators = 8L,   softmax_temperature = 0.9,   balance_probabilities = FALSE,   average_before_softmax = FALSE,   training_set_limit = 10000,   version = NULL,   control = control_tab_pfn(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'recipe' tab_pfn(   x,   data,   num_estimators = 8L,   softmax_temperature = 0.9,   balance_probabilities = FALSE,   average_before_softmax = FALSE,   training_set_limit = 10000,   version = NULL,   control = control_tab_pfn(),   ... )

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: x Depending context: data frame predictors. matrix predictors. recipe specifying set preprocessing steps created recipes::recipe(). ... currently used, required extensibility. y x data frame matrix, y outcome specified : data frame 1 numeric column. matrix 1 numeric column. numeric vector regression factor classification. num_estimators integer ensemble size. Default 8L. softmax_temperature adjustment factor divisor exponents softmax function (see Details ). Defaults 0.9. balance_probabilities logical adjust prior probabilities cases class imbalance. Default FALSE. Classification . average_before_softmax logical. cases num_estimators > 1, average done using softmax function ? Default FALSE. training_set_limit integer greater 2L (possibly Inf) can used keep training data within limits data constraints imposed Python library. version character string model version (e.g., "v2", "v2.5"). NULL (default), Python library's current default version used. set, model initialized via create_default_for_version() corresponding ModelVersion enum value. control list options produced control_tab_pfn(). formula formula specifying outcome terms left-hand side, predictor terms right-hand side. data recipe formula used, data specified : data frame containing predictors outcome.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: tab_pfn object elements: fit: python object containing model. levels: character string class levels (NULL regression) training: vector training set dimensions. logging: R python messages produced computations. blueprint: object produced hardhat::mold() used process new data prediction.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: model can used without graphics processing unit (GPU). However, fairly limited used CPU (GPU). might additional data size limitation warnings CPU computations, , understandably, execution time much longer. CPU computations can also consume significant amount system memory, depending size data. GPUs using CUDA (Compute Unified Device Architecture) effective. Limited testing others shown GPUs Metal Performance Shaders (MPS) instructions (e.g., Apple GPUs) limited utility specific computations might slower CPU data sets.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: Starting version 2.5, using TabPFN requires accepting model license obtaining token PriorLabs. model version (v2.5, v2.6, etc.) license must accepted individually. set access: Visit https://ux.priorlabs.ai create account. Go License tab accept license model version intend use. Obtain token account page. Set TABPFN_TOKEN environment variable. easiest way add .Renviron file: usethis function edit_r_environ() can helpful . Users already TABPFN_TOKEN set can use TabPFN v2 without additional steps.
code: TABPFN_TOKEN=your_token_value

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: need working Python virtual environment correct packages use modeling functions. least two ways proceed.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: first approach, strongly suggest, simply load package attempt run model. prompt reticulate create ephemeral environment automatically install required packages. process look like : location environment can found tools::R_user_dir("reticulate", "cache"). See documentation reticulate::py_require() learn method.
code: > library(tabfm)   >   > predictors <- mtcars[, -1]   > outcome <- mtcars[, 1]   >   > # XY interface   > mod <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome)   Downloading uv...Done!   Downloading cpython-3.12.12 (download) (15.9MiB)    Downloading cpython-3.12.12 (download)   Downloading setuptools (1.1MiB)   Downloading scikit-learn (8.2MiB)   Downloading numpy (4.9MiB)    <downloading and installing more packages>     Downloading llvmlite    Downloading torch   Installed 58 packages in 350ms   > mod   TabPFN Regression Model    Training set   i 32 data points   i 10 predictors

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: Alternatively, can use functions reticulate package create virtual environment install required Python packages . example pattern : virtual environment installed, can declare preferred Python installation use_virtualenv(). (must reticulate initialized Python, .e., attempting use tabpfn):
code: library(reticulate)    venv_name <- "r-tabpfn"    # exact name can be different   venv_seed_python <-     virtualenv_starter(">=3.11,<3.14")     virtualenv_create(     envname = venv_name,     python = venv_seed_python,     packages = c("numpy", "tabpfn")   ) reticulate::use_virtualenv("r-tabpfn")

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: default, limits training data dimensions: Version 2.0: number training set samples (10,000) , number predictors (500). unchangeable limit number classes (10). Version 2.5: number training set samples (50,000) , number predictors (2,000). unchangeable limit number classes (10). Predictors require preprocessing; missing values factor vectors allowed.

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: default, TabPFN uses Python library's current default model version. two ways override .

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: Use version argument select specific released model version. example:
code: # Use version 2.0   mod <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome, version = "v2")    # Use version 2.5   mod <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome, version = "v2.5")

what: Pointing to a local model file
title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: model file disk (e.g., downloaded offline use), pass path via control_tab_pfn(model_path = ...): Note version model_path mutually exclusive: version set, overwrites model_path supplied control.
code: ctrl <- control_tab_pfn(model_path = "/path/to/model_file.ckpt")   mod  <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome, control = ctrl)

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: softmax_temperature value, softmax terms : value softmax_temperature = 1 results plain softmax value.
code: exp(value / softmax_temperature)

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
text: Hollmann, Noah, Samuel Müller, Lennart Purucker, Arjun Krishnakumar, Max Körfer, Shi Bin Hoo, Robin Tibor Schirrmeister, Frank Hutter. "Accurate predictions small data tabular foundation model." Nature 637, . 8045 (2025): 319-326. Hollmann, Noah, Samuel Müller, Katharina Eggensperger, Frank Hutter. "Tabpfn: transformer solves small tabular classification problems second." arXiv preprint arXiv:2207.01848 (2022). Müller, Samuel, Noah Hollmann, Sebastian Pineda Arango, Josif Grabocka, Frank Hutter. "Transformers can Bayesian inference." arXiv preprint arXiv:2112.10510 (2021).

title: Fit a TabPFN model. — tab_pfn
code: predictors <- mtcars[, -1] outcome <- mtcars[, 1]  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (is_tab_pfn_installed() & interactive()) {  # XY interface  mod <- tab_pfn(predictors, outcome)   # Formula interface  mod2 <- tab_pfn(mpg ~ ., mtcars)   # Recipes interface  if (rlang::is_installed("recipes")) {   suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(recipes))   rec <-    recipe(mpg ~ ., mtcars) %>%    step_log(disp)    mod3 <- tab_pfn(rec, mtcars)   mod3  } } } # }

what: tabfm: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R — tabfm-package
title: tabfm: Foundation Model Wrappers for Tabular Data in R — tabfm-package
text: Provides consistent R API classification regression tabular foundation models, including 'TabPFN' 'TabICLv2'. calculations served via 'Python' 'reticulate'.

what: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
title: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
text: 2026-05-05, 36 pre-trained models equaling roughly 1.2 GB storage. model trained various synthetic & real datasets tailored classification & regression. function routine require sign one-time license 2.5 & 2.6 model varieties. Downloading models take time.

title: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
code: tabpfn_download_models(cache_dir = NULL)

title: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
text: cache_dir option override default cache directory

title: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect downloading model files.

title: Download all TabPFN pre-trained model checkpoints — tabpfn_download_models
code: # \donttest{ tabpfn_download_models() #> Warning: Some Python package requirements declared via `py_require()` are not installed in the selected Python environment: (/home/runner/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python) #>   tabicl # }

what: List available TabPFN model versions — tabpfn_list_versions
title: List available TabPFN model versions — tabpfn_list_versions
text: Returns character vector valid model version strings accepted tab_pfn()'s version argument. available model versions queried directly currently installed Python tabpfn library, hard-coded package, results may differ across Python library versions.

title: List available TabPFN model versions — tabpfn_list_versions
code: tabpfn_list_versions()

title: List available TabPFN model versions — tabpfn_list_versions
text: character vector model version strings.
